chat_messages.text: How does NegEx handle nested negation?
NegEx uses rule-based trigger terms and scope windows. Nested negation like 'not sure if no pneumonia' is handled by tracking double-negatives explicitly.
What are the main limitations of that approach?
Rule-based systems are brittle on out-of-vocabulary trigger terms and degrade on informal clinical notes. Scope window size is also a tuned constant.
Is there a neural alternative?
Yes. BioNLP-based models like BioBERT fine-tuned on i2b2 NegEx can reach 95%+ F1 and generalise better across note types.
Which dataset should I use for evaluation?
The 2010 i2b2/VA challenge dataset is standard. It covers discharge summaries with gold negation and uncertainty spans.
How do I run hybrid search in LanceDB 0.17?
LanceDB 0.17 supports hybrid search via rerankers. Create a table, add a full-text index, then call query().vector(...).text(...) and pass a reranker.
Which reranker should I use locally without an API?
Use RRFReranker for pure reciprocal rank fusion — no model needed. CohereReranker or CrossEncoderReranker require network or GPU respectively.
Can I store sparse BM25 vectors in the same table?
LanceDB's FTS index handles sparse retrieval internally. You don't store BM25 vectors explicitly; the engine builds the inverted index on the text column.
Got it. What is the default distance metric for dense vectors?
Why use frozen=True on dataclasses?
frozen=True makes instances hashable and prevents accidental mutation, which is important for DTOs passed through multi-step pipelines.
Does it work with mutable default fields like lists?
No. You must use field(default_factory=list) for list fields and field(default_factory=dict) for dict fields. The frozen constraint prevents post-init reassignment too.
Can I still use __post_init__ with frozen dataclasses?
Yes, but to set derived attributes you must call object.__setattr__(self, 'field', value) inside __post_init__ since direct assignment raises FrozenInstanceError.
Is NamedTuple a better alternative for simple value objects?
NamedTuple is lighter and always immutable, but lacks default_factory and __post_init__. Use frozen dataclass when you need those; NamedTuple for simple coordinates or keys.
